title: Apple iPad 11" A16 128GB Wi-Fi $479 (C&C Only) @ The Good Guys ($455.05 Price Beat @ Officeworks)
body:
Deal: Apple iPad 11" A16 128GB Wi-Fi $479 (C&C Only) @ The Good Guys ($455.05 Price Beat @ Officeworks)

[rpb] Wow those new prices are insane. Get the old price from retailers before they adjust their price!
[shxhshzhz] Not sure. For those who want to try, $455 after price beat
[pennypincher] $479 still, you have to choose click and collect and the extra discount will be applied
[asspray] mac mini m4 has jumped in price. $1299 for 16/512???
[freewhere] Is it worth upgrading from iPad 8th Gen?
[Shrew] Depends what you use it for.
A12 vs A16 with the newer design and a slightly larger screen, but otherwise the base model iPad is still, well, basic.
[foojm] It depends on your use case
The A16 base model has USB-C, faster CPU, 2x the memory, and 4x the storage. It'll also get IOS updates for longer.
[mrmaltalian] Before you pull the trigger, just remember:
It does not support Apple Intelligence.
The display is not laminated, so writing with the Pencil doesn't feel quite as premium as on the iPad Air.
It is limited to a 60Hz refresh rate.
It doesn't support Stage Manager.
If you're ok with those compromises, then you're good to go!
[ThighHighDemise] It doesn't support Stage Manager.
?
I thought every iPad does from iOS 26 - I multitask on my iPad mini 5…
[mrmaltalian] You're right to question that.
As of iPadOS 26, the 11-inch iPad (A16) does support Stage Manager. Apple expanded Stage Manager beyond the M-series iPads, and the A16 iPad is on the official list of supported models.
There is one important limitation though:
Extended desktop on an external monitor. When connected to a display, the iPad (A16) can only mirror its screen. True extended display via Stage Manager remains limited to newer M-series iPads.
[rpb] The display being not laminated makes it cheaper to repair though so thats why they can be good for school kids
[billbob123454] Thanks finally found a reason to upgrade my iPad Air 2 from 10 years ago.
[hozj] Looks like Flybuys is doing 10x points at Officeworks for EOFY (for met at least).
So if you're lazy, you'd net 4,950 points (~$30) which would bring down OW $495 to effectively $465.
[rpb] Well 4950 flybuys points is $24.75 so that would make it $470.25. That’s still less than this now expired TGG deal if Officeworks don’t adjust prices in the morning.
Note the $499 at Big W for the ‘Big Toy Sale’ is showing Sold Out on all colours except blue and even then in-store only. So I think Big W deal is over before the sale ends!
